content: Fixing a major computer system crash alone late at night, especially when it was your mistake.
Leaving law school to open an art center in a poor neighborhood.
Deleting a popular social media account because keeping up with it is too tiring.
Taking a prestigious office job to please your family, even though you dislike strict workplace rules.
Making an app more addictive to increase profits, even if it is bad for young users.
Spending days organizing thousands of rows of messy data for a science experiment.
Presenting an unusual fashion design to a panel of strict judges.
Leaving your role as a robotics team leader to focus on writing code behind the scenes.
Moving to another country alone with just one bag and an unstable freelance job.
Rewriting a single paragraph for hours until it sounds exactly right.
Taking an unpaid job at a famous company just to put their name on your resume.
Answering urgent phone calls at a crisis center to help people in trouble.
Turning down a high-paying corporate job to start a risky tech company in a garage.
Following laboratory instructions exactly to avoid ruining an expensive chemical mixture.
Spending your summer vacation repairing large engines at an auto shop.
Planning a public environmental protest, even if your family gets angry at you.
Sharing company shares equally with your team, even though you did most of the work.
Setting up a computer program to trade stocks automatically without human help.
Delaying choosing a college major so you can study many different subjects.
Living alone in a remote cabin for months to write and draw a graphic novel.
Changing the way you speak, dress, and act to fit in with wealthy students.
Spending weeks painting a mural on a wall that will soon be torn down.
Running a secret business writing school papers for others, despite the risk of getting expelled.
Spending months in a physics lab trying an experiment that keeps failing.
Letting your parents choose your college classes to avoid arguments, even if you hate the subject.
Standing on a cold street corner trying to sell a new product to passersby.
Managing a large warehouse where a computer system times and tracks the workers.
Giving up a large family inheritance because it requires you to work at the family business for years.
Spending your weekend setting up the wiring and sensors for an indoor greenhouse.
Helping a company succeed behind the scenes while letting a coworker take the credit.
Leading a rescue team into a collapsed cave during a storm to find lost hikers.
Spending months alone reading and translating very old, damaged papers.
Helping angry neighbors settle an argument over their property boundary.
Writing a long and complicated business plan that must follow strict legal rules.
Designing financial software that helps wealthy clients pay less in taxes legally.
Checking thousands of paper receipts to find a small banking mistake.
Dancing freely without a script in front of a panel of theater directors.
Leaving a high-level manager job to focus on writing code and analyzing data.
Hitchhiking alone across a continent without any plans or maps.
Spending weeks carving and tuning the wood parts inside an acoustic guitar.
Doing boring office work at a famous fashion company just to impress people at parties.
Treating severely injured patients quickly in a busy hospital emergency room.
Starting a risky green energy company instead of taking a high-paying job in oil.
Operating a machine to cut metal parts with perfect accuracy during a long shift.
Digging trenches and laying heavy pipes for a farm watering system in the heat.
Leading an unofficial workers' strike at a large warehouse, despite the risk of being fired.
Giving away your medical software for free to the public instead of selling it for millions.
Building a system to route heavy internet traffic quickly and smoothly.
Taking a mix of science, literature, and hands-on classes instead of a business degree.
Spending months in the woods building a wooden cabin by hand using basic tools.
Changing your opinions and behavior just to fit in with an exclusive club.
Building metal water sculptures that are designed to rust and fall apart over a year.
Selling copied blueprints illegally to foreign companies for cryptocurrency, despite the risk of arrest.
Spending two years in a lab testing many different versions of a medicine that keep failing.
Finishing a boring business degree to please your parents, while ignoring what you actually want to do.
Presenting a bold, expensive plan for clean city buses or trains to a skeptical local government.
Managing a large recycling center that uses automated systems to sort waste.
Giving up your family's shipping business to work as an underwater diver repairing boats.
Building and adjusting a custom device to help take clear photos of stars.
Reorganizing a charity behind the scenes while letting someone else take the public credit.
